mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If a sustained period of positive RSAFS month-over-month growth (e.g., >0.2% for 3 consecutive months) coincides with negative 20d SPX returns for more than 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds a critical threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that typically precedes an economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as investors anticipate lower future earnings.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS consistently above 5.0% leads to positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling unemployment (UNRATE) and strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a tightening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving S&P 500 returns positively over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by 0.1% month-over-month AND PAYEMS grows by >100k in the last month.
failure_condition: If periods of simultaneous falling UNRATE and strong PAYEMS growth are associated with negative 20d SPX returns more than 40% of the time over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low interest rates, an increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals strong economic activity and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon due to immediate market optimism.
failure_condition: If HOUST increases during low interest rate regimes do not correlate with positive 5d SPX returns, or if the correlation is negative for more than 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its long-term average indicates a contraction in the manufacturing sector, signaling broader economic weakness that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines consistently below this threshold are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in over 50% of cases over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to a negative outlook for equities and thus negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual growth consistently above 3% is associated with positive 60d SPX returns for more than 12 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and potential recession, causing immediate investor panic and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more in a single month.
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases of 0.3pp or more are followed by positive 5d SPX returns in more than 30% of occurrences over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which boosts investor confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: If periods meeting both GDPC1 and RSAFS growth conditions consistently result in negative 20d SPX returns for more than 9 months.
